title: Shaggy British Chocolate Victoria Sponge
description: A classic Welsh dessert featuring a moist sponge cake, rich plain chocolate, and a hint of nostalgia.
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F) and grease two 20cm (8 inch) round cake tins.
Cream together butter and granulated sugar until light and fluffy.
Beat in vanilla extract and eggs, one at a time.
In a separate bowl, whisk together flour and caster sugar.
Add the dry ingredients to the butter mixture and mix until just combined.
Melt plain chocolate in a double boiler and fold into the cake mixture.
Divide the mixture evenly between the prepared tins and smooth the tops.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until a skewer inserted into the centre comes out clean.
Allow the cakes to cool in the tins for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.
Once the cakes are completely cool, place one on a serving plate and spread a layer of melted chocolate on top.
Place the second cake on top and dust with caster sugar.
Serve at room temperature, dust with additional caster sugar if desired.
